project.name: Tugas VAR
project.context: Virtual dan Augmented Reality dengan pendekatan WebAR

designPhilosophy: Make the WebAR coffee object read as a small physical cafe item anchored to the Hiro marker. Preserve the static HTML, CSS, JavaScript, A-Frame, and AR.js structure already present in the repository.

distinctiveMoves: Use the GLB demitasse model with a visible manual liquid surface, subtle highlight, and faint steam so the marker object reads as a filled coffee cup.

conceptualAnchor.anchorReference: hand-thrown ceramic demitasse on a cafe saucer

derivedTokenLogic.anchorReference: hand-thrown ceramic demitasse on a cafe saucer
derivedTokenLogic.colorDerivationSource: Roasted coffee browns define liquid depth, warmer ceramic browns define the cup body, lighter tan defines rim and saucer separation, and translucent white defines steam.
derivedTokenLogic.spacingDerivationSource: The cup is scaled around the Hiro marker rather than the viewport, keeping the saucer low and the cup compact so tracking remains readable.
derivedTokenLogic.typographyDerivationSource: AR labels stay compact and secondary because object recognition is the primary visual task.
derivedTokenLogic.motionDerivationSource: Rotation preserves a shallow side-view tilt while steam uses slow low-opacity upward motion to suggest heat without fighting marker tracking.
derivedTokenLogic.validationRule: The marker object must read as a small coffee cup before it reads as an abstract cylinder or circle.

motionPaletteDecision.densityDecisionSource: Use low-density motion because camera tracking and object recognition are more important than decorative animation.
motionPaletteDecision.spatialDecision: 3D is the primary product surface because the menu item must appear anchored above the Hiro marker.

aiSafeUiAudit.failureDefinition: UI is AI-safe when it relies on predictable card stacks, rounded template panels, generic abstract logos, decorative grid wallpaper, beige or slate safety palettes, soft glow backgrounds, or first-output composition with only local copy swapped in.
aiSafeUiAudit.interchangeabilityTest: If this UI can be renamed from Tugas VAR to another product category without changing composition, palette, iconography, and motion language, the design is genericity drift and must be revised.
requiredProductSpecificSignals: GLB cup model with manual coffee surface, highlight, and steam
aiSafeUiAudit.paletteExplorationRule: Use a visually exploratory product-derived palette while preserving WCAG contrast and status clarity; do not use readability as an excuse for cream, slate, monochrome, or gradient defaults.
aiSafeUiAudit.backgroundPatternRule: Lines, grids, scanlines, noise, glows, blobs, logos, and geometric decoration must serve a named product function such as alignment, measurement, navigation, crop guidance, timeline reading, status, or motion continuity.

aiColorAudit.failureDefinition: AI color drift happens when a palette uses safe or fashionable defaults before deriving semantic roles from the product anchor.
aiColorAudit.reviewQuestion: Why does this palette belong to this product instead of a generic first-pass AI UI?

motionSpatialCourageAudit.defaultStance: Treat motion, scroll choreography, canvas, WebGL, and 3D as first-class options; omit them only with a product, content-density, performance, accessibility, or device reason.
motionSpatialCourageAudit.rejectionRule: Do not mark 3D/canvas unnecessary by habit; state the product reason and the replacement interaction quality.
motionSpatialCourageAudit.reviewQuestion: Did the agent make the experience as expressive as the product can responsibly support?

aiSafeUiAudit.reviewQuestion: What at-a-glance visual evidence proves this screen belongs to this product and not a generic AI-generated template?

libraryDecisions.purpose: The current fix uses the local GLB cup model plus small A-Frame overlay primitives for liquid, highlight, and steam.
libraryDecisions.fallbackIfUnavailable: Use native CSS, browser APIs, or existing project dependencies until live verification is available.

colorTruth.intent: Choose semantic palette roles from the product context "Virtual dan Augmented Reality dengan pendekatan WebAR", current repo evidence, and accessibility needs. Do not inherit fixed palettes or generic SaaS color defaults from this scaffold.

mutationRules.mobile: Define a mobile-specific composition with reordered, merged, or disclosed content where appropriate. Scale-only shrink behavior is failure.
mutationRules.tablet: Define a tablet-specific regrouping strategy rather than a width-only reduction of desktop.
mutationRules.desktop: Define a desktop composition that uses space intentionally and avoids generic equal-weight modules unless the project evidence justifies them.

motionSystem.purpose: Default to a modern motion plan for UI work: use expressive transitions, spatial choreography, micro-interactions, and motion libraries when they strengthen hierarchy, continuity, feedback, memorability, or perceived product quality. If implementation needs a motion library, the LLM must choose a current compatible option from official docs instead of relying on an offline default.

viewportBehavior.mobile: Recompose the experience for touch, task priority, and constrained attention. Mobile should be a deliberate mobile design, not a shrunken desktop.
viewportBehavior.tablet: Regroup surfaces for medium-width use, preserving task clarity without cloning either desktop or mobile blindly.
viewportBehavior.desktop: Use the available space to improve hierarchy, scanability, and interaction quality without defaulting to template grids or generic dashboard chrome.

designExecutionHandoff.primaryExperienceGoal: Define the main fullstack product journey for Tugas VAR from repo evidence, the brief, and current docs. The seed must not prescribe final layout.

surfacePlan.goal: Choose the first task or reading path from product evidence and reject template shells.

mobile.rationale: Define a mobile-specific composition with reordered, merged, or disclosed content where appropriate. Scale-only shrink behavior is failure.

tablet.rationale: Define a tablet-specific regrouping strategy rather than a width-only reduction of desktop.

desktop.rationale: Define a desktop composition that uses space intentionally and avoids generic equal-weight modules unless the project evidence justifies them.

interactionStateMatrix.notes: Refine states from project language and the conceptual anchor; do not use anonymous default panels.

taskFlowNarrative: Entry: define how Tugas VAR starts the journey from real evidence, not a generic opener.
Resolution: define proof, feedback, recovery, and next action without leftover template chrome.
designExecutionHandoff.signatureMoveRationale: Agent must choose one project-specific visual, motion, typographic, or interaction move and explain why generic fallback weakens it.

creativeCommitment.failureMode: generic quality words without a named real-world reference are not enough

dimensions.question: Does the UI feel authored and project-specific rather than like a default framework or template kit?
Does the changed UI still follow the explicit design contract, interaction priorities, and accessibility boundaries?
Do typography, spacing, color usage, and component behaviors still feel like one system?
Does the UI preserve task clarity, feedback quality, and user confidence in the touched flows?
Does motion act as part of the design language while staying purposeful, performant, reduced-motion-safe, and consistent with the product tone?

contextHygiene.approvedExternalConstraintUsage: Convert explicit user-supplied external constraints into current-project rules without comparing against or imitating the source surface.

experiencePrinciples: Design must feel project-specific, not interchangeable with generic SaaS templates.
Readable UI must still avoid AI-safe template look; safety is not a substitute for authored visual direction.
Major interface decisions must be explainable in product and user terms.
Accessibility, responsiveness, and implementation realism are non-negotiable.
Accessibility is the floor, not a reason to erase visual personality or fall back to AI color defaults.
Cross-viewport behavior must reorganize tasks and navigation, not just scale the desktop layout down.
A single agent-chosen conceptual anchor must unify typography, spacing, morphology, motion, and responsive composition when user research is absent.
Expressive motion and spatial interaction are expected for modern UI work when they support the anchor; keep them accessible and performant instead of suppressing them by default.
When the user asks for a zero-based redesign, existing UI becomes content and behavior evidence only; prior visual DNA must be discarded unless explicitly approved.
requiredDesignSections: Spacing, Layout Rhythm, and Density Strategy
